specs.title: Overview
specs.description: What HASP is, what ships in the public repo, and how the local broker keeps secrets out of coding-agent context while still letting tools run.

specs.title: Quickstart
specs.description: The shortest safe path to a working local HASP install: set up the vault, capture a secret, bind a repo, and prove one brokered run end to end.

specs.title: Mental model
specs.description: How to think about HASP: vaults, repo boundaries, consumers, grants, sessions, audit, and redaction, and how the pieces fit into one model.

specs.title: Agent mental model
specs.description: How coding agents use HASP handles, MCP tools, grants, and brokered runs to get real work done without ever reading a raw secret value.

specs.title: Value-free manifests
specs.description: The committed .hasp.manifest.json contract that declares requirements, targets, examples, delivery, and safety rules without storing any secret.

specs.title: Install
specs.description: Install, upgrade, and uninstall HASP through Homebrew, plus packaged release downloads and building the broker from source on macOS and Linux.

specs.title: After Install
specs.description: The common first-run path after installing HASP: initialize the vault, run setup, and confirm the daemon and keychain are working.

specs.title: Command guide
specs.description: What each HASP command does, when to reach for it, and how it compares to nearby commands, organized so you can find the right one fast.

specs.title: Operator Guide
specs.description: Environment variables, repo guardrails, audit logging, backup and restore, and the practices that keep a local HASP install safe to operate.

specs.title: Telemetry
specs.description: How opt-in CLI telemetry works in HASP: what the payload contains, consent and retention rules, how to erase data, and the privacy guarantees.

specs.title: Repo targets
specs.description: Value-free repo requirements, target-scoped secret delivery, worked examples, and how app and MCP targets resolve inside a bound project.

specs.title: Credential sets
specs.description: Typed manifest sets for coupled credentials: atomic vault items, schema-checked roles, reviewed target delivery, and MCP-safe metadata.

specs.title: V1 production guide
specs.description: Run HASP in production: the local broker, OS keychain, background daemon, secret import, repo binding, and whole-program verification, end to end.

specs.title: Agent Profiles
specs.description: What a first-class agent profile guarantees, the expectations each one meets, and the generic broker path for agents without a profile.

specs.title: Codex CLI
specs.description: Connect Codex CLI to HASP as a brokered MCP surface so it can run with project secrets without ever reading their plaintext values.

specs.title: Claude Code
specs.description: Connect Claude Code to HASP as a brokered MCP surface so it can use project secrets without ever reading their plaintext values.

specs.title: Cursor
specs.description: Connect Cursor to HASP as a brokered MCP surface so the editor can run with project secrets without ever reading their plaintext values.

specs.title: Aider
specs.description: Connect Aider to HASP as a brokered MCP surface so the agent can run with project secrets without ever reading their plaintext values.

specs.title: Hermes
specs.description: Connect Hermes to HASP as a brokered MCP surface so the agent can run with project secrets without ever reading their plaintext values.

specs.title: Pi (Inflection AI)
specs.description: Connect Pi to HASP through a generated package extension and brokered MCP surface, so it runs with project secrets without reading their values.

specs.title: OpenClaw
specs.description: Connect OpenClaw to HASP as a brokered MCP surface so the agent can run with project secrets without ever reading their plaintext values.

specs.title: Generic Broker Path
specs.description: Use HASP with any CLI- or MCP-capable agent that has no first-class profile yet, through the generic broker path that injects secrets safely.

specs.title: Changelog
specs.description: Public release notes for shipped HASP versions, with the changes, fixes, and security updates included in each tagged release.

specs.title: Release distribution
specs.description: How HASP releases are distributed: build assets, hosted mirrors, checksums, signatures, and the Homebrew formula update flow operators rely on.

specs.title: Install and release
specs.description: Operator-facing release verification and install details, including how to confirm checksums and signatures before trusting a HASP binary.

specs.title: Glossary
specs.description: The HASP vocabulary used across CLI help, documentation, audit events, and error messages, defined in one place so terms stay consistent.

specs.title: CLI reference
specs.description: The exact help output generated by the installed hasp binary, covering every command, flag, and subcommand currently available.

specs.title: Errors
specs.description: HASP error messages, stable exit-code buckets, JSON error codes, the hints they carry, and recovery guidance for each failure mode.
